disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden overnight eruption of hundreds of small red teardrop/dot-shaped spots over the trunk (abdomen and back)
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe the rash and where it is?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They came up all of a sudden overnight — hundreds of tiny red teardrop-shaped dots all over my tummy and back.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A sore throat one week ago treated with a completed course of oral penicillin, finished three days before the rash
trigger_question_examples: Have you taken any new medicines lately?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I had a bad sore throat last week — the doctor said it was strep — and I took a course of penicillin, which I finished three days ago.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The spots are mildly itchy but not painful
trigger_question_examples: Are the spots itchy or sore?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're a little itchy but they don't hurt.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No lip or tongue swelling, no shortness of breath, no wheeze at present
trigger_question_examples: Is there any swelling of your lips, tongue or throat?
Any trouble breathing, wheeze or tightness in your chest?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my lips and tongue aren't swollen and I'm breathing completely normally.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, and the spots fade when a glass is pressed against them (blanching)
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a fever or feel hot and shivery?
If you press a clear glass firmly on the spots, do they fade or stay the same?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I don't have a fever, and when I press a glass on them they fade and go pale.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The spots have a fine flaky, slightly scaly surface
trigger_question_examples: Are the spots flat, raised, blistered or scaly?
Is there any flaking or scale on top of them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, they're a bit flaky on top, like a fine dry scale.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient is frightened this is an allergic reaction to the antibiotic
trigger_question_examples: What are you most worried this could be?
Do you have any concerns about the medicines you took?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm really scared it's an allergic reaction to the penicillin I was on.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new soaps, detergents, foods or other environmental exposures
trigger_question_examples: Have you used any new soaps, detergents, foods or products recently?
Any new exposures at home or work around the time this started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing new — same soap, same washing powder, no new foods.
